steps.title: Find the probability for room C using complementary probability
steps.content:
Since every visitor is in exactly one of three rooms, the probabilities must sum to 1. We have:

$$P(\text{A}) + P(\text{B}) + P(\text{C}) = 1$$

Substituting the given probabilities:

$$0.68 + 0.24 + P(\text{C}) = 1$$

$$P(\text{C}) = 1 - 0.68 - 0.24 = 0.08$$

steps.title: Convert probability to visitor count
steps.content:
The number of visitors in room C equals the probability of room C times the total number of visitors:

$$\text{Number in C} = P(\text{C}) \times \text{Total} = 0.08 \times 375$$

$$0.08 \times 375 = \frac{8}{100} \times 375 = \frac{3000}{100} = 30$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
